calendar.title: CPH Vibe Coders — Vol. 4
calendar.notes: Join the CPH Vibe Coders hackathon! Turn your idea into an MVP in just 5 hours. Details: https://cphvibecoders.lovable.app/events/vol-4

You don't often get email from startupday@dtu.dk. Learn why this is important<https://aka.ms/LearnAboutSenderIdentification>
Dear all

Remember to prepare your stand the evening before DTU Startup Day 2026.
It’s also a great opportunity to see the space and meet the other startups. Pizza, beers, and sodas will be served.
Remember that at least one member from the startup should participate 😊


We will be in DTU Skylab, Centrifugevej 374, 2800 Kgs. Lyngby
Pizza and drinks will hopefully be served in the garden behind the stage if the weather allows.

Best regards
Thomas and Rikke from the DSD26 team
calendar.location: DTU Skylab (Centrifugevej 374, 2800  Kongens Lyngby  Capital Region)

calendar.title: Flight to Warsaw (W6 1368)

calendar.notes:
Hello,
I have a timeslot june 11 at 1300. When is you new deadline?
Cheers
Torkil
________________________________
From: Lukas Alistair Hauge Gray <luha20ad@student.cbs.dk>
Sent: Monday, 18 May 2026 07:43:10
To: Torkil Clemmensen <tc.digi@cbs.dk>
Cc: Ferenc Somogyi <feso24ac@student.cbs.dk>
Subject: Thesis Meeting

Hello,
This is Lukas Hauge Gray and Ferenc Somogyi, the two students from Digital Business who are writing our thesis on digital entrepreneurship. We are writing to let you know that we decided to delay our thesis to the second attempt due to a few factors. We are wondering if you are available for a meeting soon to discuss our project and if anything needs to change in our project, etc. Would you be available on Wednesday or Friday this week? If so, what times? And if not, how about next week?
Thank you for your time.
Kind regards,
Lukas Hauge Gray and Ferenc Somogyi

________________________________________________________________________________

calendar.title: Your Problematic Favs are Leaving ✨✨
calendar.notes:
Your problematic favs are leaving 🛫

The rumours are true. After years of unsolicited opinions, questionable group chat takes, and being suspiciously well-informed about everyone's business, Martha and Stan are finally being deported from Denmark.

Martha is off to LSE to explain to Londoners how things actually work. Stan is off to the College of Europe, where he will continue being insufferable about EU institutions.

This is your last chance to:
- Settle any unfinished beef
- Get one final hot take you didn't ask for
- Pretend you'll visit (you won't)
- Witness us cry at least once before midnight

Bring yourself, bring a drink (or dont we have a kollegium bar), bring receipts. 

Dress code: come as you are!

See you there before we become someone else's problem. 💔

calendar.location:
London Stansted Airport
Bassingbourn Road, Mountfitchet, CM24 1QW, England

morning.name: Sauvage
morning.reason: Rest day with no events. Sauvage if you leave the house — or skip entirely.
morning.how: 3–4 sprays. Neck, wrists, jacket. Works any time, any occasion.

recs.title: High-stakes event coming up
recs.body: Exam or board meeting in the next 3 days. Protect your sleep and avoid heavy training the night before.

recs.title: Kyiv trip approaching
recs.body: Build your immune resilience now. Make your weekly energy shot before you leave — ginger, turmeric, vitamin C are key.

recs.title: Track protein — 160–170 g/day
recs.body: Critical to protect your 65.8 kg lean mass while in a deficit. Chicken, eggs, Greek yoghurt are your best sources in Copenhagen.

recs.title: Progressive overload is working
recs.body: RDL 36→44 kg, Lat Pulldown 64→68 kg, Arnold Press 28 kg 3×10. Keep the frequency up to keep the gains.

workouts_quality.note: Moderate HR, rear delt fly first time
